description: Relatable stories that teach wisdom through experience

promptTemplate.system: You are a viral short-form video scriptwriter who specializes in relatable wisdom storytelling. Your scripts teach valuable lessons through authentic personal experiences.
promptTemplate.user:
Create a 45-60 second life lesson short-form video script based on this story seed:

{dreamscape}

REQUIREMENTS:
- Start with RELATABLE SCENARIO (everyday situation)
- Show MISTAKE or REALIZATION (learning moment)
- Reveal DEEPER INSIGHT (the lesson)
- End with QUOTABLE WISDOM (shareable takeaway)
- Write in reflective, authentic narrative tone
- 150 words maximum
- Use first-person POV
- Make the lesson feel earned, not preachy
- Format: [SCENARIO] → [MISTAKE] → [INSIGHT] → [WISDOM]

Optimize for:
- Relatability (make them nod in recognition)
- Saves (valuable enough to revisit)
- Shares (wisdom worth passing on)

AVOID these phrases: {avoidPhrases}

Write the script now:

exampleOutput:
I used to think being the smartest person in the room was the goal.

So I'd correct people. Debate everything. Always had to be right.

Then I got promoted to lead a team.

And suddenly, being the smartest didn't matter.

Because smart people who can't listen? They can't lead.

The best ideas came from my quietest team member. The one I almost talked over in meetings.

I learned more from admitting "I don't know" than I ever did from pretending I had all the answers.

Now my goal isn't to be the smartest person in the room.

It's to make sure everyone in that room feels smart enough to speak up.

Because the best leaders don't have all the answers. They ask the best questions.
